FLOODING IS ONGOING AROUND WINONA. COUNTY ROAD 230 HAS BEEN BLOCKED OFF NORTH OF WINONA TO THE LOGAN DUE TO WATER OVER THE ROAD. WATER WAS 100 TO 150 YARDS WIDE AND FAIRLY DEEP. FLOODING WAS ALSO ONGONING IN WINONA...WATER WAS COMING UP INTO YARDS AND MAY HAVE COME UP INTO A FEW HOMES. LOCAL RAIN GAUGES MEASURED UP TO 5 INCHES OF RAIN FELL.

*** 1 INJ *** 5 SEMI ACCIDENT REPORTED 3 MILES NORTH OF TRIBUNE BY KANSAS HIGHWAY PATROL. ACCIDENT OCCURED DURING A DUST STORM. ONE DRIVER WAS TRANSPORTED DUE TO AN INJURY. THIS WAS THE BEGINNING OF NEAR ZERO VISIBILITY ACROSS GREELEY COUNTY.

*** 2 INJ *** REPORTED BY CO-OP OBSERVER. VISIBILITY WAS LESS THAN ONE QUARTER OF A MILE AT 2 PM. THIS WAS AROUND THE TIME OF THE PEAK WINDS AT BURLINGTON AIRPORT. IN ADDITION...INTERSTATE 70 WAS CLOSED FROM BURLINGTON TO LIMON COLORADO FROM 2 PM MDT TO 730 PM MDT DUE TO MULTIPLE ACCIDENTS...HIGH WINDS AND DUST STORM CONDITIONS. HIGHWAY 385 WAS ALSO CLOSED BETWEEN IDALIA AND CHEYENNE WELLS. 2 INJURIES OCCURRED IN ACCIDENTS NEAR MILE MARKER 429 ON INTERSTATE 70.
NEAR-ZERO VISIBILITY ON K-27 AT SHERMAN/WALLACE COUNTY LINE - OFFICER CAN BARELY SEE ACROSS THE ROAD

NEAR-ZERO VISIBILITY ON K-27 AT SHERMAN/WALLACE COUNTY LINE - OFFICER CAN BARELY SEE ACROSS THE ROAD. HIGHWAY 27 WAS CLOSED FROM GOODLAND TO TRIBUNE DUE TO THE BLOWING DUST AND AN ACCIDENT. ACCIDENT OCCURRED ON K-27 AT MILE MARKER 156...INVOLVED TWO CARS AT 407 PM MDT...NO INJURIES REPORTED.
NEAR-ZERO VISIBILITY ALONG THE CO/KS BORDER SINCE AROUND 1130 AM MDT

NEAR-ZERO VISIBILITY ALONG THE CO/KS BORDER SINCE AROUND 1130 AM MDT. TWO NON-INJURY ACCIDENTS OCCURRED DUE TO DUST STORM ON K-96. FIRST ACCIDENT WAS AT MILE MARKER 3.5 AND INVOLVED 3 CARS. SECOND ACCIDENT WAS AT MILE MARKER 3.9 AND INVOLVED 2 CARS. K-96 WAS CLOSED FROM CHEYENNE WELLS COLORADO TO TRIBUNE KANSAS.
NEAR ZERO VISIBILITY IN BLOWING DUST REPORTED BY LAW ENFORCEMENT. THIS WAS JUST WEST OF THE STATELINE. THERE WAS NO ESTIMATE OF THE WIND SPEED.
